stories.headline: Democratic socialist Kiros defeats longtime incumbent in Colorado primary
stories.summary: Former lawyer Melat Kiros, 29, has criticised Democrats for support of Israel during its genocidal war on Gaza.

stories.summary: Melat Kiros’s victory is latest challenge to Democratic Party leaders ahead of crucial US midterm elections

stories.summary: Phil Weiser beat Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary, presenting himself as the stronger foil for President Trump.

stories.headline: Spain records second-hottest June on record with nearly 900 heat-related deaths
stories.summary: Spain's meteorological agency Aemet has branded June "extremely hot", the second-warmest since 1961. The heat is estimated to have caused about 900 deaths, according to the MoMo monitoring system.

stories.headline: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
stories.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

stories.headline: Germany woos Trump with plan to make US weapons in Europe
stories.summary: Talks on ‘joint production concepts’ accelerate ahead of Nato summit

stories.headline: Goldman Flags Up Oil Surplus Even as Nations Rebuild Stockpiles
stories.summary: The global oil market is set to swing back into oversupply as the impact of the Iran war fades and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

stories.headline: Bullish July Is Just a Matter of Timing: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: A country already in crisis since the removal of its leader earlier this year by the US, now has to find a way to rebuild with little state presence in evidenceThe shaking seemed to come from nowhere. In a moment captured by fishers off Venezuela’s Caribbean coast, two earthquakes struck seconds apart. stories.headline: World’s oceans experience hottest June ever, scientists say more heat ahead
stories.summary: European Union monitors say the first half of 2026 was 'marked by sustained and exceptional ocean warmth'.

stories.headline: Iran says it couldn’t export a ‘single barrel of oil’ during US blockade
stories.summary: Iran was unable to export any oil during the US blockade of its ports, the country's chief negotiator has said.

stories.summary: Argentina's Messi is level on goals with France's Mbappe as World Cup favourites look to power through the knockouts.

stories.summary: Fans in San Diego erupt in celebration as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup knockout match, ending a 40-year drought in the competition.

stories.headline: Ukrainian volunteers in Poland support front despite growing war fatigue
stories.summary: A December survey by CBOS, an independent Polish research centre, found that 48% of Poles support accepting Ukrainian refugees, the lowest level since the start of the war.

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
stories.summary: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

stories.headline: Perpetual Rejects $1.7 Billion Takeover Bid From PE Firm EQT
stories.summary: Perpetual Ltd has rejected a takeover offer from a company indirectly controlled by Swedish private equity firm EQT AB, after the Australian fund manager’s shares surged Wednesday on speculation of a deal.

stories.headline: Eni, Mercuria to Form Global Energy Commodities Trading Venture
stories.summary: Italian oil company Eni SpA and commodities merchant Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed a deal to trade energy together globally.

stories.headline: Japan’s FX Chief Flags Contact With US, Intervention Impact
stories.summary: Japan’s currency chief suggested intervention was an effective strategy and flagged close communication between Tokyo and Washington over foreign exchange, in comments made as the yen hovers near a four-decade low.

stories.headline: Xi Positions China’s Ruling Party as Global Force for Progress | The China Show 7/1/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Jardine Matheson Taps Ex-Indonesian Fund CIO as Country Head
stories.summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings has hired Christopher Ganis, formerly chief investment officer at sovereign wealth fund Indonesia Investment Authority, as Indonesia country head, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Europe’s Bank Bulls See More Upside After 21% Quarterly Gain
stories.summary: Following a stellar second-quarter rally for European banks, investors expect one of the region’s hottest equity trades to keep delivering.

stories.headline: Abu Dhabi Proposes New Offshore-Oil Pricing
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co., the UAE’s biggest oil company, is proposing a new pricing methodology for the sale of crude supplies to term customers, opting for an approach that’s more aligned with wider regional trading norms, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter. Bloomberg's Anthony di Paola reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Afghan defence ministry says it carried out 'air strikes' in Pakistan's Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces.

stories.headline: Africa can finally mine, beneficiate and industrialise on its own terms
stories.summary: From Kenya to Ghana to Mali, governments are insisting their minerals be processed at home before they leave.

stories.headline: EU slaps €3 duty fee on SHEIN, Temu and AliExpress imports
stories.summary: A new €3 customs duty takes effect across the EU on 1 July, ending years of tax-free shopping from platforms like SHEIN, Temu and AliExpress and signalling the end for ultra-cheap online hauls. Watch the video.

stories.summary: The ruling striking down President Donald Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship reaffirmed that the 14th Amendment automatically confers citizenship on any child born in the U.S.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes in female school and college sports

stories.headline: Why are South Korean fans so angry at their football coach?

stories.headline: Stocks Fall, Dollar Climbs as Focus Turns to Warsh: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Equities started July on a cautious note as investors awaited comments from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh at a time when traders are ramping up bets on US interest rate hikes. The dollar rose.

stories.summary: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and the Iran war exposes supply concerns.

stories.headline: 4 Warning Signs for Graham Platner. And 4 for Susan Collins Too.
stories.summary: The Maine race, which both parties see as key to winning control of the Senate, is extremely close, a Times/Portland Press Herald/Siena poll found. Both candidates have reasons for concern.

stories.headline: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
stories.summary: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

stories.headline: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
stories.summary: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.

stories.headline: What to know about the looming  deadline on North American free trade
stories.summary: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

stories.headline: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption

stories.headline: Gold Drops for Third Day as Jitters Over US Rate Outlook Worsen
stories.summary: Gold fell for a third day as signals the Federal Reserve may move to tighten policy aided the dollar and traders tracked US-Iran talks.

stories.headline: Homegrown AlphaGrep Takes on Jio BlackRock With India Algo Funds
stories.summary: High-speed trader AlphaGrep Securities Pvt.’s asset management unit will offer algorithmic-driven trading strategies to India’s retail investors, according to a top executive, pitting it against the likes of Jio BlackRock Asset Management Pvt. for a slice of the nearly $900 billion mutual fund industry.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

stories.headline: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
stories.summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
stories.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.
